channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Controlling Tool Access with APIM MCP Gateway
item.description: If you've started working with MCP servers in GitHub Copilot, Claude, or any other agent host in an enterprise environment, you've probably hit a similar problem. You want to give your developers access to a useful MCP server, but not every tool it ships with. Maybe one tool is noisy and burns...
item.dc:creator: samcogan

item.title: Enterprise-ready Claude Desktop with Entra ID, APIM, and Microsoft Foundry (No Backend Required)
item.description: How I put corporate sign-in in front of Claude Desktop without writing a single line of backend code. TL;DR — In this post, I show how to securely enable Claude Desktop in enterprise environments using Microsoft Entra ID, Azure API Management, and Microsoft Foundry — without deploying a custom...
item.dc:creator: LZhang

item.title: Scaling High-Performance CAD on Azure Virtual Desktop with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000
item.description: Overview of Siemens NX Multi-Session on Azure This validation study evaluates how Siemens NX 2506 performs in a multi-user AVD environment, using an RTX-based Azure VM. The focus was to test: 15 and 30 concurrent users Single-host vs multi-host configurations Consistent workloads under identical...
item.dc:creator: Sunita_AZ0708

item.title: Intelligent sampling in Microsoft Foundry: the science behind selecting better production traces
item.description: Authors: Ilya Matiach, Morteza Ziyadi, José Santos, Ali Mahmoudzadeh, Shuo Qiu, Salma Elshafey, April Kwong, Vivek Bhadauria TL;DR Microsoft Foundry's intelligent sampling feature (used when creating an evaluation or fine-tuning dataset from production agent traces) uses a MinHash farthest-first...
item.dc:creator: imatiach

item.title: ICMP Support for Azure StandardV2 NAT Gateway
item.description: Our customers, across all industries, depend on consistent outbound connectivity and observability to operate and troubleshoot their cloud workloads at scale. As Azure environments grow in size and complexity, teams increasingly rely on standard network diagnostic tools such as ping to quickly...
item.dc:creator: malaikanazim

item.title: Anatomy of the change
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl Incidents, alerts, correlation, and data—what actually changes with the new platform, and why it works in your favor. When you open Microsoft Sentinel in Microsoft Defender for the first time, the shift feels immediate:...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1

item.title: Benchmarks in Microsoft Foundry (preview): Standardized model and agent quality checks
item.description: Authors: Ilya Matiach, Morteza Ziyadi, Han Che, José Santos, Ali Mahmoudzadeh, Shuo Qiu, Salma Elshafey, April Kwong, Vivek Bhadauria Introduction Benchmarks in Microsoft Foundry (preview) make that kind of measurement a first-class part of the development workflow. You can run well-known...
item.dc:creator: imatiach

item.title: Introducing New Additions to Microsoft Sentinel Normalization and ASIM
item.description: TL;DR: New ASIM parsers for Azure Firewall, Key Vault, AWS CloudTrail (EC2, S3, IAM), and 10+ third-party products. Two new schemas — Asset Entities and AI Agent Events. Plus changelogs on GitHub and a heads-up on an upcoming breaking change in ProcessEvent parsers. What's New Security teams deal...
item.dc:creator: derricklee

item.title: When your agent extensions fight each other
item.description: This is the fourth article in a series about Agent Experience (AX): the practice of making AI coding agents work correctly with your technology. The series covers what you can and can’t control in the agent stack, how to measure whether your extensions are helping or hurting, and how to iterate...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: How to Score a User Simulator: Introducing USR-8
item.description: Authors: José Santos, Shuo Qiu, Morteza Ziyadi User simulators have become a standard part of the agent-building toolkit: cheaper than real-user pilots, faster than scripted dialogues, and the only way to push an agent through hundreds of plausible conversations between every code change. The hard...
item.dc:creator: jcasantos

item.title: Azure Backup for Azure Cosmos DB Public Preview Adds Immutable Backups and Long-Term Retention
item.description: Azure Backup for Azure Cosmos DB Public Preview Adds Immutable Backups and Long-Term Retention Picture the first few hours after a serious data incident. A production application is down. Security teams are still trying to understand what happened. Application owners need to know which recovery...
item.dc:creator: Hans Wieser [MS], Jay Gordon

item.title: Build an Automated SLA Risk Agent with Routines in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: What if your support team could start every morning with an AI-generated summary of tickets most likely to breach SLA? This tutorial shows you how to build exactly that: a grounded agent in Microsoft Foundry that automatically analyzes your ticket data and surfaces risks. Using Routines in...
item.dc:creator: LauraVerghote

item.title: Security Copilot RBAC for Embedded Experience in Unified Security Platform
item.description: Introduction The evolution of Security Operations Centers (SOC) is increasingly driven by AI-powered capabilities that improve efficiency, accuracy, and response time. Microsoft Security Copilot represents a significant advancement in this space by embedding AI-driven assistance directly within...
item.dc:creator: SantoshPargi

item.title: Azure Sets a New Performance Record for LLM Training Benchmark at Extreme Scale
item.description: Azure achieved the most performant MLPerf Training v6.0 result to date for Llama 3.1 405B, with a time-to-train of just over seven minutes according to MLCommons. This loadbearing benchmark measures how communication overhead and system stability dominate training performance, where Azure’s full...
item.dc:creator: azinheidarshenas

item.title: Closing the loop on container security: From code to runtime in the AI era
item.description: Containers are the backbone of modern cloud-native apps — and increasingly, the infrastructure powering AI, from AI assistants to a new wave of intelligent agents. They also blur the line between build, deploy, and runtime: a single code change can become a running workload in minutes. A...
item.dc:creator: mahersko

item.title: Azure Databricks at Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026: updates and new announcements
item.description: Databricks Data + AI Summit brings together the global data and AI community in San Francisco to share product news, technical breakthroughs, and customer stories. This year, as usual, we have a lot of Azure Databricks announcements, a strong presence across the event, and a continued focus on...
item.dc:creator: AnaviNahar

item.title: Azure Arc Server May 2026 Forum
item.description: Please find the recording for the monthly Azure Arc Server Forum on YouTube! During the May 2026 Azure Arc Server Forum, we discussed: Private Preview of Windows Server 2016 Extended Security Updates (ESUs): Customers with Windows Server 2016 Servers with an NDA and willingness to provide feedback...
item.dc:creator: Aurnov_Chattopadhyay

item.title: Announcing Public Preview: Agent Identities Asset Connector for Microsoft Sentinel
item.description: As organizations accelerate adoption of AI agents across Microsoft 365 and enterprise environments, security teams face a fundamental shift: Agents are becoming first-class identities and securing them requires understanding both their behavior and their identity context. We are excited to announce...
item.dc:creator: Krishna_Sagar_B_V

item.title: Aspire in VS Code: the 13.4 developer loop
item.description: Aspire 13.4 brings more of the local development loop into VS Code: AppHost CodeLens actions, live resource state, dashboard shortcuts, typed resource command prompts, TypeScript AppHost support, and multi-language debugging. The post Aspire in VS Code: the 13.4 developer loop appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Adam Ratzman

item.title: Transform your security operation with a unified experience in Defender
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl Security operations teams today are being asked to do more than ever: respond faster, manage increasing data volumes, reduce operational complexity, stay ahead of evolving threats, and balance cost and efficiency. That’s why...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1

item.title: Competing against yourself
item.description: You shipped a new CLI: better developer experience, modern architecture, and optimized for agents. You deprecated the old one, updated the docs, and blogged about it. Developers are migrating. Then someone asks an AI coding agent to scaffold a project, and the agent… uses the old tool. Training...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: The Case for an Ontology Layer in Telecoms
item.description: The Challenge: Data Without Context in the Telco AI Era GenAI and analytics tools and solutions pull data from applications and from downstream and external data sources. However, when data is decontextualized from the applications where it was created, it often loses the business meaning,...
item.dc:creator: Alberto_Manuel

item.title: Announcing General Availability of the Azure Cosmos DB Built-in Connector for Logic Apps Standard
item.description: Today, we’re excited to announce the general availability of the Azure Cosmos DB built-in connector for Azure Logic Apps Standard. This connector gives you a native, high-performance way to integrate Azure Cosmos DB into your Standard logic app workflows, with better throughput, lower latency, and...
item.dc:creator: Theo van Kraay

item.title: Network security perimeter for Azure Service Bus & also now available in Azure Gov. Regions
item.description: TL; DR Network security perimeter support for Azure Service Bus is now Generally Available. With this, you can now place your Service Bus namespace inside a central security boundary and apply perimeter-based governance for inbound/outbound network access—while keeping key PaaS-to-PaaS scenarios...
item.dc:creator: shashankamalladi

item.title: Azure Function App — Queue-Based Architecture for Long-Running Sync Jobs
item.description: The Problem: HTTP Triggers and Long-Running Jobs Don't Mix Here's a situation you've probably run into: you have a job that needs to loop over dozens of Azure resources, call APIs, and do real work. You wrap it in an HTTP-triggered Azure Function so it can be called on demand. It works great and...
item.dc:creator: Jamesdld23

item.title: A Guided Tour of the New Microsoft Foundry Labs
item.description: There's a moment every developer chases — the first time you take something genuinely at the edge of what's possible and watch it run on your screen, against your problem. Microsoft Foundry Labs exists to give you that moment on repeat. This is the place where Microsoft's most ambitious AI research...
item.dc:creator: vaidyas

item.title: ArchAngel: Skilling the next developer generation for the Agentic transformation.
item.description: AI is transforming the SDLC at speed but there's a quieter question following close behind. If the code is being written for your junior developers, when do they learn the skills to become senior ones and how can they use these tools without losing understanding of what they've made? Most tools try...
item.dc:creator: RohitMadhavKrishnan

item.title: Make Visual Studio look the way you want
item.description: Themes are personal. Some of us live in dark mode, some swear by high contrast, and some of us have very strong opinions about that one shade of blue from years ago. The new themes in Visual Studio 2026 are built on Fluent, which gives us a much more consistent and accessible foundation, but we […]...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: TLS Certificate Pinning and Best Practices in Azure Open-Source Relational Databases
item.description: TLS certificate pinning in Azure Database for PostgreSQL and MySQL Transport Layer Security (TLS) encrypts data in transit between client applications and the server and authenticates the service endpoint in client-server authentication. Azure Database server certificates are issued by well-known...
item.dc:creator: TameikaL

item.title: Automating Daily MDE Compliance Monitoring Across Azure VMs
item.description: The Problem We’re Solving Most security teams have no automated way to know when a VM silently falls out of MDE coverage, whether because the agent stopped, the VM was newly provisioned without onboarding, or the device stopped reporting. This Logic App closes that gap and puts the right...
item.dc:creator: SayanRoy

item.title: Agents That Test Agents: A Cloud-Native Skill-Eval Harness on Foundry Hosted Agents
item.description: Skills are an agent's must-have. So test them. A skill is the lightest way to give an agent durable, reusable behavior: a SKILL.md file you author once, store centrally in Foundry's versioned Skills API, and inject into a Hosted Agent's context — no code change, no redeploy. That's why skills have...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: Deploy Anyscale on Azure with Terraform: a step-by-step guide
item.description: Deploy Anyscale on Azure end-to-end with Terraform, AKS managed Gateway API, Istio-based app routing, and the AzAPI provider for newly released services.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How to Measure Token Impact of MCP Tool Invocation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Your MCP-enabled agent just ran, and the token counts don't add up. The API says 773 tokens. The portal trace shows 581/141. The trajectory view displays something else entirely. Before you file a bug report, here's what's actually happening—and how to build an evidence pattern that makes...
item.dc:creator: vrajakishore

item.title: Troubleshooting ML Model Loading, GPU Issues, and Memory Pressure in Azure Container Apps
item.description: Introduction Deploying an AI application to Azure Container Apps is fundamentally different from deploying a web API. When you containerize a Django REST API, the application starts in a few seconds, the memory footprint is predictable, and the CPU usage scales linearly with requests. When you...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs
item.description: Real-world lessons from evolving a production chatbot into a coordinator-based multi-agent architecture, including performance trade-offs for enterprise-scale agent reuse. The post Orchestration Patterns for Multi-Agent Systems: Performance and Trade-offs appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lily Jia

item.title: June Patches for Azure DevOps Server
item.description: We are releasing new patches for our self‑hosted product, Azure DevOps Server. We strongly recommend that all customers stay up to date with the latest, most secure version of Azure DevOps Server. The most recent release, Azure DevOps Server, is available on the download page. The following...
item.dc:creator: Gloridel Morales

item.title: What to Do When You Hit Capacity in Azure Databricks: Engage, Mitigate, Plan!
item.description: Microsoft's Cloud Architects: Paul Singh PaulSingh​, Eduardo Dos Santos eduardomdossantos​, Chris Walk cwalk​, Peter Lo PeterLo​, Tim Orentlikher tim_orentlikher​, Ajmal Hossain ajmalhossain​, Chris Haynes Chris_Haynes​, and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Start Here: Engage Microsoft Capacity constraints...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Transitioning from Azure Language Features to Foundry Models
item.description: Azure AI Language in Foundry Tools will begin deprecating eight features: Key Phrase Extraction, Entity Linking, Sentiment Analysis & Opinion Mining, Summarization (document & conversation), Conversational Language Understanding (CLU), Custom Question Answering (CQA), Orchestration Workflow, and...
item.dc:creator: renaliu

item.title: Your agent just scaffolded a project from 2020
item.description: Your agent ran a scaffold command. Project generated, dependencies resolved, no errors. Everything looks fine. Except it’s based on the project structure from 2020, and neither you nor the agent noticed. How npx picks the right-but-wrong version When an agent scaffolds a project or runs a CLI tool,...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: Copilot Autofix for GitHub Advanced Security for Azure DevOps
item.description: Over the last few years, we’ve encouraged customers to move their repositories from Azure Repos to GitHub, where the newest AI-powered and agentic development experiences land first. Migrating isn’t equally simple for everyone. A move to GitHub can range from straightforward to a multi-year...
item.dc:creator: Laura Jiang

item.title: Azure Functions at Build 2026 Update
item.description: Azure Functions took another big leap at Build 2026. It is now the best programming model for event-driven apps and agents, on the best infrastructure to write secure code that scales. The headline features: serverless agents, connectors to M365, Teams, and more, Go, MCP, and Durable Tasks....
item.dc:creator: nzthiago

item.title: Review pull requests without leaving Visual Studio
item.description: Pull request integration in Visual Studio has been one of the most requested Git features. Developers have been asking for a way to open a PR, inspect the changes, discuss feedback, and finish the review without switching to the browser. The feedback on that request has played a big role in shaping...
item.dc:creator: Leah Tran

item.title: Real-Time Simulation with Ansys Discovery on Azure AMD Radeon V710 GPUs
item.description: Overview of Synopsys-Ansys Discovery Ansys Discovery is an interactive engineering application that unifies 3D geometry modeling, real‑time simulation, and visual analysis into a single, immersive workspace. It is purpose‑built to accelerate early‑stage engineering exploration while maintaining...
item.dc:creator: Sunita_AZ0708

item.title: Microsoft Fabric to Azure Databricks: Another Better Together Story!
item.description: Author's: Oscar Alvarado oscaralvarado​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Note: This article describes a solution idea. Your cloud architect can use this guidance to help visualize the major components for a typical implementation. Use this article as a starting point to design a well-architected solution...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Dynamic Connection Properties in Azure Logic Apps Standard
item.description: Switching Built-in Connections at Runtime The Problem Imagine your organization has a single Logic App workflow used by multiple teams. Each team has its own SFTP server, database, or service bus namespace. Traditionally, you'd need separate workflows or hardcode a single connection — neither...
item.dc:creator: ShubhamSachdeva99

item.title: Debug App Startup Faster on Azure App Service for Linux with Startup Logs
item.description: When an app fails to start on Azure App Service for Linux, one of the first things you need is visibility into what happened during startup. This can include container initialization, runtime setup, startup command execution, application output, and warmup probe results. To make this easier, we...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: Analyse Intune Diagnostics Logs with GitHub Copilot
item.description: Hello everyone! I’m Stefan Röll, Cloud Solution Architect at Microsoft Germany for Cloud Endpoints. I work closely with IT Admins to improve their experience with everything around Intune, W365 and Windows. I know that troubleshooting an issue can be time consuming and challenging. In this blog I...
item.dc:creator: StefanRöll

item.title: Performance Tuning Cold Starts, Scaling Delays, and Startup Latency in Azure Container Apps
item.description: Introduction There is a particular kind of frustration that comes not when your application fails to start, but when it starts too slowly. The container is running, the health probes pass, your monitoring shows green — and yet every few minutes a user somewhere in the world hits a request that...
item.dc:creator: BhaktiRath95

item.title: At-Scale Failure Reporting for Azure Update Manager
item.description: Introduction Azure Update Manager simplifies patching across Azure virtual machines and Azure Arc-enabled servers by providing a centralized platform for patch assessment and installation. However, as environments scale, a key challenge emerges—efficiently identifying and troubleshooting patch...
item.dc:creator: rajeshkumar30

item.title: Now in Foundry: Command A+ (W4A4), Chandra OCR 2, and GLM-OCR
item.description: We are seeing two distinct trends this week. The first is around how low-bit quantization has developed to the point where large reasoning models can fit on a single accelerator with less quality loss. Second, a new wave of OCR-specialized vision-language models are redefining the...
item.dc:creator: Osi
